questions.question: Explain the five stages of the Product Life Cycle in the context of Green IT and sustainable software design.
keyPoints: Requirements & Design: Consider sustainability from the start, eco-requirements gathering
Development: Green coding practices, efficient algorithms, sustainable architecture
Deployment: Energy-efficient hosting, virtualization, optimal resource allocation
Use/Maintenance: Minimize runtime energy, optimize queries, lazy loading strategies
Retirement: Data sanitization, hardware recycling, responsible e-waste disposal

questions.question: What is Carbon Footprint in Green IT? How can software developers reduce the carbon footprint of applications?
keyPoints: Carbon footprint: Total GHG emissions in CO2 equivalent caused by IT product/service
Reduction strategies: Energy-efficient algorithms, server optimization, renewable energy hosting
Code optimization, data center selection, carbon-aware computing, reducing data transfer

questions.question: Differentiate between 'Embodied Energy' and 'Operational Energy' in the IT product lifecycle.
keyPoints: Embodied Energy: Energy consumed in manufacturing, transporting, and disposing of hardware
Operational Energy: Energy consumed during the actual use/operation of software
Embodied is upfront cost; operational is continuous over product lifetime
For software, operational energy typically dominates (60-90% of total)

questions.question: What is PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness)? A data center reports PUE of 1.3. If IT equipment consumes 500 kWh, calculate total energy consumption and identify the overhead energy.
keyPoints: PUE = Total Facility Energy / IT Equipment Energy
Total Energy = PUE × IT Energy = 1.3 × 500 = 650 kWh
Overhead = Total - IT = 650 - 500 = 150 kWh (cooling, lighting, etc.)
Lower PUE indicates better energy efficiency

questions.question: List and explain three strategies for sustainable software disposal or retirement.
keyPoints: Data Sanitization: Securely erase all data before disposal to prevent breaches
Hardware Recycling: Send to certified e-waste recyclers for material recovery
Refurbishment/Reuse: Donate or resell functional equipment to extend lifespan
Responsible E-waste Disposal: Use certified facilities to prevent toxic leakage
Virtual Machine Migration: Migrate workloads to newer, more efficient servers

questions.question: What is Green Coding? Provide three practical examples of green coding practices.
keyPoints: Green Coding: Writing code that minimizes energy consumption and resource usage
Examples: Efficient algorithms (O(n) vs O(n²)), minimizing database queries with caching
Lazy loading (load resources only when needed), using energy-efficient data structures
Reducing network calls through batching, optimizing memory allocation patterns

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'Energy Profiling' and its importance in sustainable software development.
keyPoints: Energy Profiling: Measuring energy consumption at component/module level
Tools measure power draw during execution to identify energy hotspots
Importance: Enables targeted optimization where it matters most
Helps developers understand which code sections consume most energy
Supports data-driven decisions for sustainability improvements

questions.question: Discuss the environmental impact of software across all stages of its Product Life Cycle. Explain why the 'Use' phase is typically the most significant and suggest strategies to minimize environmental impact during this phase.
keyPoints: All stages: Manufacturing (embodied energy), Development (resources for tooling), Deployment (infrastructure), Use (dominant - 60-90% impact), Disposal (e-waste)
Use phase significance: Long runtime duration, continuous electricity consumption, cooling needs, server farms operate 24/7
Strategies: Energy-efficient algorithms, server virtualization, cloud optimization, renewable energy
Code optimization, data center PUE reduction, carbon-aware scheduling, caching to reduce computation
Client-side vs server-side tradeoffs, mobile app battery optimization, efficient network protocols

questions.question: A company is developing a new web application. From a sustainable software design perspective, outline the considerations and best practices they should implement at each stage of the Product Life Cycle to minimize environmental impact.
keyPoints: Design: Eco-requirements, sustainability goals, green architecture decisions, energy-aware design patterns
Development: Green coding standards, efficient algorithms, minimal dependencies, optimized queries
Deployment: Green hosting providers, CDN usage, right-sized servers, virtualization/containerization
Use: Lazy loading, caching, efficient APIs, minimizing data transfer, energy-efficient frontend
Retirement: Data sanitization, migration planning, hardware recycling policies, migration to efficient platforms
